narration: Define a class named Addition. Inside the class, create a method add that takes two integer arguments n1 and n2 and returns their sum.
Create a second method named add that takes three integer arguments n1, n2, and n3 and returns their sum.
Create a third method named add that takes four double arguments n1, n2, n3, and n4 and returns their sum.
Instantiate an object of the Addition class and call the add method with two integer arguments 10 and 2.
Create a new instance of the Addition class and call the add method with three integer arguments 5, 6, and 5.
Create another instance of the Addition class and call the add method with four double arguments 8, 9, 2, and 3.
Print the results of the three add methods to the console.
Explain that method overloading allows multiple methods with the same name but different parameters, resolved at compile time.
Define a parent class named ClassA with a void method show that prints 'This is A'.
Define a child class named ClassB that extends ClassA and overrides the show method to print 'This is B'.
Instantiate an object of ClassB and call the show method.
Comment out the show method in ClassA to demonstrate that the child class method is executed at runtime.
Explain that method overriding allows a child class to provide a specific implementation of a method already defined in the parent class, resolved at runtime.
Explain that the super keyword can be used to access the parent class method while overriding.
Summarize that polymorphism in Java includes compile-time behavior via method overloading and runtime behavior via method overriding.
